content: Existing Self-Refine frameworks, which rely on single-perspective iterative self-feedback to improve output without extra training, can be augmented by integrating multiple inconsistent self-perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms to resolve the common blind spot of single-perspective feedback stagnation. This creates a hybrid framework that retains Self-Refine's key advantage of needing no supervised data or additional training, while boosting refinement performance by exposing unrecognized flaws in earlier outputs.
